Tech.title: Sustainable Denim Dyeing through Microbial Fermentation
Tech.description: Introducing the background of CutisBio's Aniline-free indigo dye development and the future of eco-friendly denim fashion.
Tech.intro: The most loved fashion item, 'Blue Denim'. However, behind its beautiful color lurked 'aniline', a petrochemical-derived substance that threatens health and the environment. CutisBio fundamentally solves this problem using microbes.
Tech.section1Title: 1. The Absolute Necessity of Being Aniline-Free
Tech.section1Text: Aniline is a hazardous substance classified as a potential carcinogen by the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) under the WHO. It is indiscriminately discharged into water resources during denim production, destroying ecosystems and adversely affecting end consumers who have long-term skin contact.
Tech.comparisonImageAlt: Infographic showing Aniline and Methylaniline chemical structures and the risks of dermatitis and bladder cancer
Tech.comparisonCaption: Chemical structures of Aniline and Methylaniline, and their critical human health threats including contact dermatitis and bladder cancer.
Tech.section2Title: 2. CutisBio's Innovation: Turning Microbes into Factories
Tech.section2Text: Nature had the answer. Using advanced synthetic biology technology, we endowed special microbes with metabolic pathways similar to plants. These microbes produce indigo precursors on their own, fundamentally blocking the use of fatal chemicals like aniline.
Tech.section3Text: Being eco-friendly doesn't mean compromising on quality. CutisBio's bio-indigo takes a 'Drop-in' form that can be immediately introduced without modifying the infrastructure of existing dyeing factories. It perfectly replicates the same color depth (Shade) and rubbing fastness as conventional chemical dyes.
Tech.certSectionText: We are proud to announce the completion of world-class certifications for various aspects of sustainability, environmental friendliness, and human safety.

faqList.question: What are the problems with traditional indigo dyeing?
faqList.answer: Conventional synthetic indigo dyes are produced using petrochemicals such as aniline and hydrogen cyanide. This causes severe water pollution and poses health threats to workers and end consumers.

faqList.question: What is microbial fermentation-based indigo dye?
faqList.answer: CutisBio's technology uses specially engineered microbes to produce indigo precursors through a natural fermentation process. By using biological mechanisms instead of chemical synthesis, we create 100% aniline-free dye.

faqList.question: Is there any difference in conventional garment quality?
faqList.answer: No. It achieves equal or superior quality, color depth, and washing/rubbing fastness compared to traditional indigo denim dyeing. We have achieved perfect sustainability without compromising quality.

Contact.title: Contact Us
Contact.description: If you have any questions about CutisBio, please leave a message and we will get back to you quickly.

ProblemSolution.problemTitle: The Hidden Cost of Our Denim
ProblemSolution.problemText: Traditional dyeing processes rely on petrochemicals, leaving severe environmental pollution and health threats. 'Aniline', in particular, is one of the most critical issues.
ProblemSolution.solutionText: 100% aniline-free dye completely devoid of toxic substances through microbial fermentation. Providing perfect sustainability safe for both humans and nature.

Process.title: Biomanufacturing Process
Process.subtitle: Advanced synthetic biology mechanism mimicking nature
Process.step1Text: Specially redesigning microbial DNA based on enzyme pathways existing in nature.
Process.step2Text: In a precise fermentation environment, microbes naturally produce indigo precursors like plants.
Process.step3Text: Completing a 100% bio-based dye through an eco-friendly purification process.
Process.step4Text: Immediately applicable to existing dyeing factory infrastructure without need for additional equipment replacement.
